faqs.question: What is Zuno Network?
faqs.answer: Zuno is a decentralised network that helps improve AI accuracy. You use your phone to review AI-generated answers and earn ZUNO tokens for doing so. Think of it as getting paid to fact-check AI.

faqs.question: What problem does Zuno solve?
faqs.answer: AI systems often produce answers that sound correct but aren't — these are called hallucinations. Zuno fixes this by having real people review AI outputs, creating higher quality, more trustworthy AI data that enterprises actually pay for.

faqs.question: Who built Zuno?
faqs.answer: Zuno was founded by Stephen, an Australian systems engineer with 20+ years of experience across Government, Telecommunications, and Healthcare IT.

faqs.question: What is DePIN?
faqs.answer: DePIN stands for Decentralised Physical Infrastructure Network. It means real people — not big tech companies — contribute their devices or time to run the network, and get rewarded with tokens in return. Zuno is a DePIN project for AI data validation.

faqs.question: Is Zuno legitimate?
faqs.answer: Yes. Zuno is a fully open project — the litepaper, codebase, and founder background are all publicly available. The founder Stephen is an Australian IT professional with a verifiable 20+ year career. There is no anonymous team.

faqs.question: How do I participate?
faqs.answer: Download the Zuno app on your Android or iOS device, create an account, and start completing AI validation tasks. Each completed task earns you points that convert to ZUNO tokens at Token Launch.

faqs.question: Do I need to know about crypto to use Zuno?
faqs.answer: No. You just need the Zuno app and a free Solana wallet to receive your earnings. Crypto knowledge helps but is not required to get started.

faqs.question: What wallet do I need?
faqs.answer: You need a free Solana wallet to receive your ZUNO tokens. Think of it like a bank account number for your earnings. We recommend one of three options: Cake Wallet (best for privacy), Phantom (most popular), or Solflare (has 24/7 live chat support if you get stuck).

faqs.question: What device do I need?
faqs.answer: Any modern Android or iOS smartphone. The Zuno app is a Progressive Web App (PWA), which means you install it directly from your browser — no App Store download required.

faqs.question: Is it free to join?
faqs.answer: Yes. Creating an account and completing tasks is completely free. Zuno Supporter Pass holders earn at a higher rate, but a Supporter Pass is never required to participate.

faqs.question: How do I earn?
faqs.answer: Complete AI validation tasks in the app — you'll be shown two AI-generated answers to the same question and asked to pick the better one, or flag both as incorrect. Each completed task earns you points.

faqs.question: What are points worth?
faqs.answer: Points convert to ZUNO tokens 1:1 at Token Launch. For example, if you have 500 points when the token launches, you will receive 500 ZUNO tokens.

faqs.question: Is there a daily earning limit?
faqs.answer: Yes. Standard contributors can earn up to 10 ZUNO per day. Supporter Pass holders earn at a multiplied rate — for example, a 3x Supporter Pass earns up to 30 ZUNO per day — by completing the same tasks as everyone else.

faqs.question: Can I lose my points?
faqs.answer: No. Your earned points are tied to your account and are safe as long as you participate honestly. There are no penalties for taking breaks or going offline.

faqs.question: When do my points convert to tokens?
faqs.answer: Points convert to ZUNO tokens at Token Launch (also called TGE — Token Generation Event). This is when the ZUNO token is officially created and distributed. We will announce the date in advance across all official channels.

faqs.question: What is the ZUNO token?
faqs.answer: ZUNO is the native token of the Zuno Network. It rewards contributors for their work, powers the governance system, and will be tradeable on exchanges after Token Launch. It is built on the Solana blockchain.

faqs.question: What blockchain is ZUNO on?
faqs.answer: Zuno is built on Solana, which is known for fast transactions and very low fees — making it practical for everyday rewards and on-chain activity.

faqs.question: What is a Token Launch (TGE)?
faqs.answer: TGE stands for Token Generation Event — it is the moment the ZUNO token is officially created and distributed to contributors. Think of it as the day your accumulated points become real, tradeable tokens. There was no presale, no private buyers, and no team allocation — everyone enters at the same starting price.

faqs.question: When will ZUNO be listed on exchanges?
faqs.answer: No confirmed date yet. Token Launch details will be announced in advance across all official Zuno channels. Follow us to stay updated.

faqs.question: What is governance?
faqs.answer: Governance means ZUNO token holders get a say in how the network evolves. Simply holding ZUNO gives you voting power. You can vote on things like reward emission rates, task reward amounts, treasury spending, and protocol upgrades.

faqs.question: When does governance start?
faqs.answer: The governance framework is introduced at Token Launch (Phase 4 of the Zuno roadmap). Voting starts off-chain using Snapshot and will move on-chain in a future phase.

faqs.question: Is my data safe?
faqs.answer: Yes. Zuno only uses the information needed to run your account and distribute rewards. Your personal data is never sold. For full details, refer to the Privacy Policy.

faqs.question: How is this different from crypto mining?
faqs.answer: Unlike mining, your contribution in Zuno does genuinely useful work — you are validating AI outputs that enterprises pay for. You are not solving arbitrary computational puzzles. This makes Zuno's rewards sustainable and tied to real-world demand.

faqs.question: How is Zuno Network funded?
faqs.answer: Zuno Network is currently self-funded through the founding team. To support continued development and network growth, Zuno is actively exploring grants from ecosystem foundations including the Solana Foundation and relevant Web3 infrastructure programs, venture capital from crypto-native investors aligned with decentralised AI and data infrastructure, and strategic partnerships with organisations that benefit from Zuno's data labelling network. Funding will be used to support core development, liquidity, and network expansion. Any material funding events will be disclosed publicly in line with Zuno's commitment to transparency.

faqs.question: Where can I get help?
faqs.answer: Join the Zuno Telegram community for the fastest response. You can also follow us on X (Twitter) or Reddit for announcements and discussions.

faqs.question: Where can I follow Zuno?
faqs.answer: You can follow Zuno across all official channels below.
